facts.fact_summary: MongoDB stock remains under the Americas Software coverage group.
facts.original_sentence: The stock remains under the Americas Software coverage group (Buy-rated).

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's Revenue was +5.8% above Consensus in F3Q26.
Atlas Revenue growth accelerated for a third consecutive quarter to +30% in F3Q26, up from +29% in F2Q26.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas Revenue growth accelerated a third consecutive quarter to +30% (from +29% in F2Q26)

facts.fact_summary: Non-Atlas revenue outperformed largely due to higher multi-year deals in F3Q26.
facts.original_sentence: Non-Atlas outperformed largely on higher multi-year deals

facts.fact_summary: Operating Margin (OpM) and Free Cash Flow Margin (FCFM) came in +800 bps and +1800 bps ahead, respectively, in F3Q26.
facts.original_sentence: OpM / FCFM came in +800 bps / + 1800 bps ahead on Revenue outperformance and higher cash collections.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB delivered a second quarter in FY26 at a Rule of 40+, which is the highest since F1Q24.
facts.original_sentence: Importantly, MongoDB delivered a second quarter in FY26 at a Rule of 40+, highest since F1Q24.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's stock moved +22% after hours, reflecting F3Q momentum.
facts.original_sentence: The +22% AH stock move reflects F3Q momentum plus F4Q Revenue and Op. Income guidance +6.6% / +59% above Street (incl. Atlas guidance +27% vs. Street +24%).

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB added +2500 new Atlas customers in F3Q26, which is stable compared to F2Q and well-above the +1800 baseline over the past three years.
facts.original_sentence: New logo activity stood out again with +2500 new Atlas customers (stable vs. F2Q) and well-above the +1800 baseline over the past three years, underscoring a strengthening PLG motion that could benefit further as MongoDB’s incremental Bay Area investments in developer awareness mature and MongoDB’s AI capabilities make their way to Community Edition.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's price as of 1 Dec 2025 close was $328.87.
MongoDB Inc. (MDB) has held a 'BUY' rating since May 14, 2021.
MongoDB's F3Q26 Revenue came in +5.8% above Consensus, driven by a +3.4% beat on Atlas.
facts.original_sentence: Revenue came in +5.8% above Consensus, driven by a +3.4% beat on Atlas

facts.fact_summary: Two-thirds of the Non-Atlas outperformance in F3Q26 was driven by multi-year deals.
facts.original_sentence: Management noted that two-thirds of the Non-Atlas outperformance (vs. high-end of guidance) was driven by multi-year deals.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's Net ARR Expansion Rate improved to 120% in F3Q26 from 119% in the prior quarter.
facts.original_sentence: Note Net ARR Expansion Rate improved to 120% from 119% in the prior Q.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas delivered +2500 net logo adds in F3Q26, stable compared to F2Q, and well-above the +1800 baseline over the last three years.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas again delivered +2500 net logo adds (stable vs. F2Q), remaining well-above the +1800 baseline over the L3Y.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas consumption growth from F1Q-3Q26 was consistent with comparable FY25 periods.
facts.original_sentence: Further, Management noted that Atlas consumption growth from F1Q-3Q was consistent to comparable FY25 periods, supporting Atlas growth acceleration.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's Gross Margin declined to 74% in F3Q26, compared to 77% in F3Q25, as Atlas mix expanded.
facts.original_sentence: On margins: GM declined to 74% (vs. 77% in F3Q25) as Atlas mix expanded.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's Operating Margin (OpM) came in +800 bps ahead of expectations in F3Q26 due to Revenue outperformance and lower-than-expected OpEx.
facts.original_sentence: That said, OpM came in +800 bps ahead on Revenue outperformance and lower-than-expected OpEx, as planned S&M and R&D investments took longer to implement and are now shifting into F4Q26 and FY27.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's Free Cash Flow Margin (FCFM) came in +1800 bps ahead of Consensus in F3Q26, driven by Op. Income outperformance and higher cash collections.
facts.original_sentence: FCFM came in +1800 bps ahead of Consensus, with Op. Income outperformance and higher cash collections driving the upside.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas is a fully-managed, multi-cloud service available across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas - the fully-managed, multi-cloud service across AWS, Azure, and GCP - has become the core monetization driver.

facts.fact_summary: The MongoDB platform spans full-text search, vector search, stream processing, data federation, time-series, and robust security, including queryable encryption.
facts.original_sentence: The platform now spans full-text search, vector search, stream processing, data federation, time-series, and robust security (including queryable encryption), collapsing moving parts and speeding time-to-value.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB’s revenue scale is now over $2.5 billion.
facts.original_sentence: While MongoDB’s revenue scale is now $2.5B+, its market share remains roughly 2%, leaving substantial runway as applications modernize, buyers standardize on managed services, and AI becomes embedded in software.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's market share remains roughly 2%.
facts.original_sentence: While MongoDB’s revenue scale is now $2.5B+, its market share remains roughly 2%, leaving substantial runway as applications modernize, buyers standardize on managed services, and AI becomes embedded in software.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas revenue has compounded at over 60% from FY19-26E to more than $1.8 billion in run-rate revenue.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas has compounded at over 60% from FY19-26E to more than $1.8B in run-rate Revenue despite native cloud and open-source alternatives, validating product–market fit and customer trust.

facts.fact_summary: In Version 8.0, MongoDB delivered 36% faster reads and 59% higher update throughput.
facts.original_sentence: In Version 8.0, MongoDB delivered considerable performance gains – 36% faster reads and 59% higher update throughput; we believe this matters as customers move larger and more business-critical systems onto Atlas.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas acts as a hub for adjacent capabilities, including full-text search, vector search, stream processing, and lightweight analytics, all running in the same environment.
facts.original_sentence: On top of the core database, Atlas now acts as a hub for adjacent capabilities: full-text search, vector search, stream processing and lightweight analytics all run in the same environment.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB is the 5th most popular database overall according to DB-Engines and the leading document database.
facts.original_sentence: MongoDB shows up as the 5th most popular database overall (DB-Engines) and the leading document database (Exhibit 8), with developer usage steady in the mid-20’s in recent Stack Overflow survey’s, not collapsing.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB is architecturally better suited for horizontal scalability, designed to distribute data and load across many servers.
facts.original_sentence: It is also architecturally better suited for horizontal scalability: MongoDB is designed from the ground up to distribute data and load across many servers, while scaling Postgres to similar levels requires additional tooling and engineering effort.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas growth has accelerated for three consecutive quarters, reaching +30% in F3Q26.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas growth has accelerated for three consecutive quarters (+30% in F3Q26, Exhibit 5)

facts.fact_summary: Atlas new logo adds are at record highs (2500+), compared to an average of 1800 from FY23-25.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas new logo adds are at record highs (2500+ vs. 1800 average from FY23-25, Exhibit 4)

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB's Net Expansion Rates are stable at 120%.
facts.original_sentence: Net Expansion Rates are stable at 120%, all of which are inconsistent with a story of mounting competitive displacement.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas Revenue has scaled past a $1.8 billion run-rate with a 60%+ CAGR from FY19–FY26E.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas Revenue has scaled past a $1.8B run-rate at a 60%+ CAGR from FY19–FY26E with sustained share gains among pure-plays (Exhibit 7)

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB was AWS’s North America Partner of the Year in 2024.
facts.original_sentence: MongoDB was AWS’s North America Partner of the Year in 2024 and Microsoft’s in 2025

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB was Microsoft’s Partner of the Year in 2025.
facts.original_sentence: MongoDB was AWS’s North America Partner of the Year in 2024 and Microsoft’s in 2025

facts.fact_summary: The SSPL license, introduced in 2018, continues to discourage direct reselling of MongoDB's latest code.
facts.original_sentence: Co-sell and marketplace momentum underscore this trajectory - MongoDB was AWS’s North America Partner of the Year in 2024 and Microsoft’s in 2025 - and the SSPL license (2018) continues to discourage direct reselling of the latest code.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas Vector Search is built into the database, allowing teams to store vectors alongside documents and run semantic, hybrid text, and filtered queries.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas Vector Search is already built into the database, letting teams store vectors alongside documents and run semantic, hybrid text and filtered queries all in one place – well-suited for RAG, semantic search, recommendations and anomaly detection.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB acquired Voyage AI in February 2025.
facts.original_sentence: The Feb. 2025 Voyage AI acquisition tightens this loop.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB is integrating Voyage’s embedding and reranking models to make high-quality retrieval a native capability of the platform.
facts.original_sentence: MongoDB is integrating Voyage’s embedding and reranking models so that high-quality retrieval becomes a native capability of the platform, not a separate service bolted on the side.

facts.fact_summary: Roughly 8,000 startups were building on the MongoDB platform as of August 2025.
facts.original_sentence: MongoDB has roughly 8,000 startups building on the platform (as of Aug. 2025)

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB has 200,000 new developer registrations monthly.
facts.original_sentence: 200K new developer registrations monthly and 30% of Atlas ARR is generated from customers with at least one AI use case.

facts.fact_summary: 30% of Atlas ARR is generated from customers with at least one AI use case.
facts.original_sentence: 200K new developer registrations monthly and 30% of Atlas ARR is generated from customers with at least one AI use case.

facts.fact_summary: MongoDB is expanding its partner set with major ecosystem players including LangChain, Galileo, Temporal, Vercel, Anthropic, and Cohere.
facts.original_sentence: MongoDB’s broader emphasis on expanding its partner set with major ecosystem players – LangChain, Galileo, Temporal, Vercel, Anthropic, Cohere – helping customers shorten time-to-production by pairing the platform with critical tools and services in the AI value chain.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas growth accelerated from +24% in F4Q25 to +30% in F3Q26.
facts.original_sentence: multi-quarter acceleration in Atlas growth from +24% in F4Q25 to +30% in F3Q26

facts.fact_summary: Self-serve net customer adds increased by +93% from F1Q-F3Q26 compared to the same period in FY25.
facts.original_sentence: At the same time, the company continues to benefit from a robust self-serve channel that feeds high-quality top-of-funnel activity; self-serve net customer adds are up +93% from F1Q-F3Q26 vs. the same period in FY25.

facts.fact_summary: 25% of customers with over $1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) were sourced through the self-serve channel.
facts.original_sentence: Note that 25% of >$1M ARR Customers were sourced through the self-serve channel and scaled to $1M+ 15% faster than those that came through other channels.

facts.fact_summary: Customers sourced through the self-serve channel scaled to over $1 million in ARR 15% faster than those from other channels.
facts.original_sentence: Note that 25% of >$1M ARR Customers were sourced through the self-serve channel and scaled to $1M+ 15% faster than those that came through other channels.

facts.fact_summary: 70% of Atlas ARR is driven by customers adopting at least one additional capability.
facts.original_sentence: customers are not just consuming the core database, but attaching additional services such as search, vector search and stream processing, effectively consolidating multiple data patterns and deepening dependence on the platform over time; 70% of Atlas ARR is driven by customers adopting at least one additional capability.

facts.fact_summary: In FY26, MongoDB initially provided an outlook for 10% Operating Margin (OpM) but subsequently raised guidance to 18% in F3Q26.
facts.original_sentence: FY26 is representative: the company initially provided an outlook for 10% OpM but subsequently raised guidance to 18% (F3Q26), largely on Revenue outperformance from both Atlas and non-Atlas.

facts.fact_summary: Atlas now represents 75% of MongoDB's total Revenue.
facts.original_sentence: Atlas now represents the clear majority of Revenue (75%) and continues to grow materially faster than the company overall (+30% in F3Q26), reflecting both ongoing app modernization and broader multi-cloud workload expansion.
